title: Mildly Briny Peruvian Potato Flatbread
description: A light and savory flatbread perfect for post-workout snacks, featuring a long-fermentation dough and a touch of brininess from Peruvian ingredients. This satvik-friendly dish is made with an egg wash for added richness.
instructions: Combine flour, salt, sugar, and yeast in a large mixing bowl.
Add mashed potatoes and water to the bowl and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it, and let it ferment in the refrigerator for 12 hours.
Divide the dough into 4 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Brush the tops of the flatbreads with an egg wash made from the beaten egg.
Sprinkle the dried aji amarillo pepper over the egg wash.
Drizzle the olive oil over the flatbreads.
Bake the flatbreads in the preheated oven for 15 minutes, or until golden brown.
